codeReview.summary: The reviewed commit did return the repository to a clean tree, but it clearly exceeded the feature's finalization-only contract by introducing substantive webhook, command-processing, persistence, migration, and writer behavior. This rerun review focuses on scope containment, and the commit did not stay within tree-state finalization.

issues.description: This feature was explicitly limited to legalizing an existing dirty tree with no new behavioral work, but the commit adds an entire new `internal/commands` package plus command parsing/execution logic for `/ai-review rerun|ignore|resolve|focus`. Shipping a 490-line new runtime package is substantive product behavior, not tree-state finalization.

The finalization commit changes webhook runtime behavior by introducing a `CommandProcessor` contract, note-event command handling, merge-request note detection helpers, and atomic note-command execution paths. Those are behavior changes on the ingress surface and violate the feature's requirement to avoid introducing new behavior beyond cleaning the tree.

The commit also extends comment-writing semantics with line-range payload generation, suggestion-block gating against project policy, and new range-position helpers. Those user-visible writeback changes belong to beta comment-extension work, not to a tree-state finalization-only feature.

Adding a new schema migration and corresponding sqlc/query updates changes the persisted data model (`review_runs.scope_json`) and is substantive implementation scope. A feature whose only purpose is to legalize pre-existing dirt should not introduce new schema behavior.

summary: Reviewed the feature metadata, prior scrutiny context, worker transcript skeleton, handoff, go-integration skill, and commit `a3a89ada788fa32b8a2217745d7dd6d17b6bb20f`. Although the worker achieved a clean working tree, the commit bundled major command-webhook, writer, and persistence behavior into a feature that was contractually limited to tree-state finalization, so this rerun review fails on scope contamination grounds.
